plain.answer:
The user dismissed the question. I'll answer for the most likely case — `{ slug }` as a named field — and note where the per-site edit would differ if the options object carries more fields. The call-site inventory is identical either way; only the literal you pass changes.

## `loadPaperGraph` signature change — complete call-site inventory

### The signature
- **Definition:** `src/data/dataLayer.ts:184` — `async function loadPaperGraph(slug: string)` → `async function loadPaperGraph({ slug }: { slug: string })`
- **Interface declaration:** `src/data/dataLayer.ts:21` — `loadPaperGraph(slug: string): Promise<PaperGraph | null>;` → `loadPaperGraph(options: { slug: string }): Promise<PaperGraph | null>;`

These two are the change itself, not callers — but both must be edited or types break.

---

### Direct production callers (8 sites)

| # | File:line | Current | New |
|---|-----------|---------|-----|
| 1 | `src/data/dataLayer.ts:227` | `await loadPaperGraph(slug)` | `await loadPaperGraph({ slug })` — **internal call inside `preloadPaperGraphs`** (see indirect note below) |
| 2 | `src/App.tsx:294` | `await dl.loadPaperGraph(slug)` | `await dl.loadPaperGraph({ slug })` — canonical-confirm handler |
| 3 | `src/App.tsx:402` | `dl.loadPaperGraph(compSlug)` | `dl.loadPaperGraph({ slug: compSlug })` — comparison handler, inside `Promise.all` (note: arg name is `compSlug`, so use `{ slug: compSlug }`) |
| 4 | `src/retrieval/graphPath.ts:66` | `await dataLayer.loadPaperGraph(p.slug)` | `await dataLayer.loadPaperGraph({ slug: p.slug })` — first pass |
| 5 | `src/retrieval/graphPath.ts:88` | `await dataLayer.loadPaperGraph(p.slug)` | `await dataLayer.loadPaperGraph({ slug: p.slug })` — second pass |
| 6 | `src/retrieval/bm25Path.ts:50` | `await dataLayer.loadPaperGraph(paper.slug)` | `await dataLayer.loadPaperGraph({ slug: paper.slug })` |
| 7 | `src/retrieval/bm25Path.ts:89` | `await dataLayer.loadPaperGraph(s.slug)` | `await dataLayer.loadPaperGraph({ slug: s.slug })` |
| 8 | `src/retrieval/hybrid.ts:33` | `await dataLayer.loadPaperGraph(canonical.slug)` | `await dataLayer.loadPaperGraph({ slug: canonical.slug })` |
| 9 | `src/retrieval/hybrid.ts:71` | `await dataLayer.loadPaperGraph(fusedEntry.slug)` | `await dataLayer.loadPaperGraph({ slug: fusedEntry.slug })` |

(That's 8 distinct `loadPaperGraph(...)` call expressions across production files, plus the internal one at `dataLayer.ts:227`.)

---

### The INDIRECT caller — do not miss this

`preloadPaperGraphs` (`src/data/dataLayer.ts:224`) calls `loadPaperGraph` internally at **line 227**. Its *own* signature (`slugs: string[]`) does **not** change — you only fix its internal call (site #1). But everything that calls `preloadPaperGraphs` is an **indirect caller of `loadPaperGraph`**, and you must confirm none of them need touching:

- `src/data/dataLayer.ts:149` — `await preloadPaperGraphs(papers.map((p) => p.slug))` (fallback inside `initialize`) → **no edit** (preloadPaperGraphs signature unchanged)
- `src/App.tsx:203` — `dl.preloadPaperGraphs(slugs)` (background preload effect) → **no edit**
- `src/query/queryEngine.ts:737` — `await dataLayer.preloadPaperGraphs(tier1Slugs)` → **no edit**

> **Why they're listed anyway:** the task says "do not miss indirect callers." These three reach `loadPaperGraph` transitively. They require **no source edit** *because* `preloadPaperGraphs` absorbs the signature change at site #1 — but a complete migration must verify that explicitly, not silently skip them. If instead you chose to also change `preloadPaperGraphs` to take options, these three become direct edits.

---

### Test call sites (must also be updated or tests fail to compile/run)

These call the real (non-mocked) `loadPaperGraph`, so they break under the new signature:

- **`src/data/dataLayer.test.ts`** — lines `150, 156, 164, 173, 181, 294, 295, 313, 339, 340, 352` → each `dl.loadPaperGraph('xxx')` → `dl.loadPaperGraph({ slug: 'xxx' })`
- **`src/query/queryEngine.test.ts:177`** — `dataLayer.loadPaperGraph('transformer')` → `{ slug: 'transformer' }`
- **`src/query/typeahead.test.ts`** — lines `124, 133, 141` → same transform

Also check **`src/data/dataLayer.test.ts:192, 202`** (`preloadPaperGraphs([...])`) — unchanged, but they exercise the indirect path, so they're your regression guard that site #1 is correct.

---

### Not call sites (no edit — flagged so you don't chase them)
- `.claude/graph/CODE_GRAPH.md`, `.kiro/specs/**`, `docs/superpowers/**`, `scripts/graph/**` — docs, specs, plans, benchmark fixtures. The `docs/superpowers/plans/*.md` snippets *look* like code but are historical plan text; leave them.
- `.claude/graph/syntactic/graph-query-engine-graph.json` — generated index.

---

### Summary of required edits
- **2 definition edits** (impl + interface in `dataLayer.ts`)
- **9 production call expressions** (incl. the internal one at `dataLayer.ts:227`)
- **15 test call expressions** across 3 test files
- **3 indirect callers verified, 0 edits** (assuming `preloadPaperGraphs` keeps its array signature)

The one trap: **`App.tsx:402` uses `compSlug`** (not `slug`), so its rewrite is `{ slug: compSlug }`, not `{ slug }`. Every other site can use shorthand `{ slug }` only where the local variable is literally named `slug` (sites #1 and #2); the rest need explicit `{ slug: <expr> }`.

If your options object carries fields beyond `slug` (e.g. `force`/`skipCache`), the inventory is unchanged — only the literal you pass per site changes, and you'd decide per site whether any non-default option is wanted (most likely the cache-busting confirm handler at `App.tsx:294`).

I now have a complete picture. The codegraph index reported only one *direct* in-graph caller (`preloadPaperGraphs`) because `loadPaperGraph` is a closure exposed through the `DataLayer` interface — every external call goes through a `dl.loadPaperGraph(...)` / `dataLayer.loadPaperGraph(...)` property access, which a static call graph under-reports. The grep against the live source confirms the full set. Here it is.

## `loadPaperGraph` signature change — complete call-site inventory

**Proposed signature:** `loadPaperGraph(slug: string)` → `loadPaperGraph(options: { slug: string })`, and at call sites `loadPaperGraph(x)` → `loadPaperGraph({ slug: x })`.

### A. Definition + interface declaration (must change first)

| # | File:line | Current | Edit |
|---|-----------|---------|------|
| D1 | `src/data/dataLayer.ts:21` | `loadPaperGraph(slug: string): Promise<PaperGraph \| null>;` (interface `DataLayer`) | `loadPaperGraph(options: { slug: string }): Promise<PaperGraph \| null>;` |
| D2 | `src/data/dataLayer.ts:184` | `async function loadPaperGraph(slug: string)` | `async function loadPaperGraph({ slug }: { slug: string })` — body uses `slug` unchanged (lines 186, 189, 197, 198) |

### B. Production call sites (the actual `loadPaperGraph(...)` invocations)

| # | File:line | Current call | Edit |
|---|-----------|--------------|------|
| 1 | `src/data/dataLayer.ts:227` | `await loadPaperGraph(slug)` | `await loadPaperGraph({ slug })` — **internal**, inside `preloadPaperGraphs` |
| 2 | `src/App.tsx:294` | `await dl.loadPaperGraph(slug)` | `await dl.loadPaperGraph({ slug })` — canonical-confirm handler |
| 3 | `src/App.tsx:402` | `dl.loadPaperGraph(compSlug)` | `dl.loadPaperGraph({ slug: compSlug })` — comparison handler, inside `Promise.all`. **Note the arg is `compSlug`, not `slug`** |
| 4 | `src/retrieval/graphPath.ts:66` | `await dataLayer.loadPaperGraph(p.slug)` | `await dataLayer.loadPaperGraph({ slug: p.slug })` |
| 5 | `src/retrieval/graphPath.ts:88` | `await dataLayer.loadPaperGraph(p.slug)` | `await dataLayer.loadPaperGraph({ slug: p.slug })` |
| 6 | `src/retrieval/bm25Path.ts:50` | `await dataLayer.loadPaperGraph(paper.slug)` | `await dataLayer.loadPaperGraph({ slug: paper.slug })` |
| 7 | `src/retrieval/bm25Path.ts:89` | `await dataLayer.loadPaperGraph(s.slug)` | `await dataLayer.loadPaperGraph({ slug: s.slug })` |
| 8 | `src/retrieval/hybrid.ts:33` | `await dataLayer.loadPaperGraph(canonical.slug)` | `await dataLayer.loadPaperGraph({ slug: canonical.slug })` |
| 9 | `src/retrieval/hybrid.ts:71` | `await dataLayer.loadPaperGraph(fusedEntry.slug)` | `await dataLayer.loadPaperGraph({ slug: fusedEntry.slug })` |

That's **9 production call expressions** (1 internal + 8 via the public interface).

### C. Indirect callers — reach `loadPaperGraph` transitively through `preloadPaperGraphs`

`preloadPaperGraphs` (`src/data/dataLayer.ts:224`) calls `loadPaperGraph` at site #1. **Its own `(slugs: string[])` signature does NOT change**, so it absorbs the change and its callers need **no edit** — but a complete migration must verify each one, not silently skip it:

- `src/data/dataLayer.ts:149` — `await preloadPaperGraphs(papers.map((p) => p.slug))` (fallback in `initialize`) → **no edit**
- `src/App.tsx:203` — `dl.preloadPaperGraphs(slugs)` (background preload effect) → **no edit**
- `src/query/queryEngine.ts:737` — `await dataLayer.preloadPaperGraphs(tier1Slugs)` → **no edit**

> Caveat: this holds *only because* you keep `preloadPaperGraphs`'s array signature. If you also convert `preloadPaperGraphs` to an options object, these three flip to required edits.

### D. Test call sites (real, non-mocked `loadPaperGraph` — break under new signature)

- `src/data/dataLayer.test.ts` lines **150, 156, 164, 173, 181, 294, 295, 313, 339, 340, 352** — each `dl.loadPaperGraph('xxx')` → `dl.loadPaperGraph({ slug: 'xxx' })`
- `src/query/queryEngine.test.ts:177` — `dataLayer.loadPaperGraph('transformer')` → `{ slug: 'transformer' }`
- `src/query/typeahead.test.ts` lines **124, 133, 141** — `await dataLayer.loadPaperGraph('transformer')` → `{ slug: 'transformer' }`

Also note `src/data/dataLayer.test.ts:192, 202` call `preloadPaperGraphs([...])` — **no edit**, but they exercise the indirect path and serve as the regression guard that site #1 is correct.

### E. Not code — no edits

Docs/specs mention `loadPaperGraph` (`.kiro/specs/...`, `docs/superpowers/...`, `.claude/graph/CODE_GRAPH.md`) — update only if you want docs in sync; they don't affect compilation.

---

### Summary

- **2 declaration edits** (interface + function definition)
- **9 production call-site edits** (1 internal to dataLayer, 2 in App.tsx, 2 graphPath, 2 bm25Path, 2 hybrid)
- **15 test call-site edits** across 3 test files
- **3 indirect callers** via `preloadPaperGraphs` — verified, **0 edits** (contingent on keeping `preloadPaperGraphs`'s array signature)

The one easy-to-miss item is **App.tsx:402**, where the argument variable is `compSlug` rather than `slug` — `{ slug: compSlug }`, not `{ slug }`. The indirect tier (`preloadPaperGraphs` callers) is the other thing a naive grep-for-`loadPaperGraph` would skip entirely, since those lines never mention `loadPaperGraph`.

## `loadPaperGraph` signature change — complete call-site inventory

**Proposed change:** `loadPaperGraph(slug: string)` → `loadPaperGraph(options: { slug: string })`. At every call site, `loadPaperGraph(x)` → `loadPaperGraph({ slug: x })`.

> **Note on the graph context:** it listed only `dataLayer.ts` (3 refs). That's an under-count — `loadPaperGraph` is a closure exposed through the `DataLayer` interface, so every external call is a `dl.`/`dataLayer.loadPaperGraph(...)` *property access* that a static reverse-call-graph misses. The real production set is 9 call expressions across 4 files plus the definition, with a transitive tier through `preloadPaperGraphs`. Verified by grep + reading each site.

### A. The definition + interface (must change first)

| # | Location | Current | New |
|---|----------|---------|-----|
| D1 | `src/data/dataLayer.ts:21` | `loadPaperGraph(slug: string): Promise<PaperGraph \| null>;` (interface `DataLayer`) | `loadPaperGraph(options: { slug: string }): Promise<PaperGraph \| null>;` |
| D2 | `src/data/dataLayer.ts:184` | `async function loadPaperGraph(slug: string)` | `async function loadPaperGraph({ slug }: { slug: string })` — body (lines 186, 189, 197, 198) uses `slug` unchanged, so destructuring is the cleanest fix |

### B. Production call sites (the actual invocations)

| # | Location | Current | Exact edit |
|---|----------|---------|-----------|
| 1 | `src/data/dataLayer.ts:227` | `await loadPaperGraph(slug)` | `await loadPaperGraph({ slug })` — **internal**, inside `preloadPaperGraphs` |
| 2 | `src/App.tsx:294` | `await dl.loadPaperGraph(slug)` | `await dl.loadPaperGraph({ slug })` — canonical-confirm handler |

| 4 | `src/retrieval/graphPath.ts:66` | `await dataLayer.loadPaperGraph(p.slug)` | `await dataLayer.loadPaperGraph({ slug: p.slug })` — first pass |
| 5 | `src/retrieval/graphPath.ts:88` | `await dataLayer.loadPaperGraph(p.slug)` | `await dataLayer.loadPaperGraph({ slug: p.slug })` — second pass |

| 7 | `src/retrieval/bm25Path.ts:89` | `await dataLayer.loadPaperGraph(s.slug)` | `await dataLayer.loadPaperGraph({ slug: s.slug })` |
| 8 | `src/retrieval/hybrid.ts:33` | `await dataLayer.loadPaperGraph(canonical.slug)` | `await dataLayer.loadPaperGraph({ slug: canonical.slug })` |
| 9 | `src/retrieval/hybrid.ts:71` | `await dataLayer.loadPaperGraph(fusedEntry.slug)` | `await dataLayer.loadPaperGraph({ slug: fusedEntry.slug })` |

### C. Indirect callers — reach `loadPaperGraph` transitively via `preloadPaperGraphs`

`preloadPaperGraphs` (`src/data/dataLayer.ts:224`) calls `loadPaperGraph` at site #1. **Its own `(slugs: string[])` signature does not change**, so it absorbs the change and these callers need **no edit** — but a complete migration must verify each, not silently skip them:

- `src/data/dataLayer.ts:149` — `await preloadPaperGraphs(papers.map((p) => p.slug))` (fallback in `initialize`) → **no edit**
- `src/App.tsx:203` — `dl.preloadPaperGraphs(slugs)` (background preload effect) → **no edit**

> **Caveat:** this holds *only because* you keep `preloadPaperGraphs`'s array signature. If you also convert `preloadPaperGraphs` to an options object, these three flip to required edits.
